primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of an intragroup dividend receivable as a hedged item for foreign exchange risk under IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: This paragraph establishes that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items, which directly governs whether intragroup items qualify.
This paragraph provides an exception for monetary items forming part of a net investment in a foreign operation, allowing foreign exchange differences to be recognized in OCI.

supporting_authority.reason: This interpretation clarifies the scope of eligible hedged items in net investment hedges, specifically addressing foreign exchange differences between functional currencies of parent and foreign operation.
Defines eligible hedged items (recognized assets/liabilities, firm commitments, forecast transactions, net investments) that form the basis for determining hedge accounting eligibility.
Addresses how intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated without showing currency fluctuation effects in consolidated financial statements.

peripheral_authority.reason: Deals with cash flow classification of interest and dividends, not with hedge accounting eligibility of intragroup items.
Addresses measurement and recognition of dividend payables for non-cash distributions, unrelated to foreign exchange hedge accounting.
Deals with related party disclosure requirements, not with hedge accounting treatment of intragroup items.

treatment_families.family: Net investment hedge accounting for foreign operation
General restriction on hedging intragroup items
Exception for intragroup monetary items in consolidated statements

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 paragraph 10 and IAS 21 paragraph 32 provide that hedge accounting may apply to foreign exchange differences arising between functional currencies of parent and foreign operation for net investments, which may encompass intragroup dividend receivables representing part of that net investment.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items to those with external parties, which would preclude hedge accounting for an intragroup dividend receivable unless it qualifies under a specific exception.

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item recognition without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 recognizes that intragroup monetary items create foreign exchange exposure that cannot be eliminated on consolidation, but this does not constitute hedge accounting—it merely permits recognition of exchange differences in profit or loss or OCI depending on circumstances.
